cluster: C6: GWAS/QTL + recent reviews + TOP1α/UPF1/TTG2 + MEE45 + SEEDSTICK + lesser-known

papers.title: Developing Genetic Engineering Techniques for Control of Seed Size and Yield
papers.authors: Tripathi RK, Aguirre JA, Singh J

papers.title: TOP1α, UPF1, and TTG2 regulate seed size in a parental dosage–dependent manner
papers.authors: Li C, Gong X, Zhang B, Liang Z, Wong CE, See BYH, Yu H

papers.title: The Arabidopsis MATERNAL EFFECT EMBRYO ARREST45 protein modulates maternal auxin biosynthesis and controls seed size by inducing AINTEGUMENTA
papers.authors: Sechet J, Frey A, Effroy-Cuzzi D, Berger A, Perreau F, Cueff G, Charif D, Rajjou L, Mouille G, North HM, Marion-Poll A

papers.title: The Arabidopsis MADS-Domain Transcription Factor SEEDSTICK Controls Seed Size via Direct Activation of E2Fa
papers.authors: Mizzotti C, Rotasperti L, Moretto M, Tadini L, Resentini F, Galliani BM, Galbiati M, Jenik PD, Tonelli C, Colombo L, Masiero S

papers.title: Maternal control of triploid seed development by the TRANSPARENT TESTA 8 (TT8) transcription factor in Arabidopsis thaliana
papers.authors: Dziasek K, Santos-Gonzalez J, Wang J, Leister D, Santos LD, Kohler C

papers.title: Genetic Interaction of SEEDSTICK, GORDITA and AUXIN RESPONSE FACTOR 2 during Seed Development
papers.authors: Ripoll JJ, Zhu M, Brocke S, Hon CT, Yanofsky MF, Boudaoud A, Roeder AHK

edges.mechanism: DNA topoisomerase maintains condensed chromatin at TTG2 locus; suppresses antipodal-cell expression
edges.evidence: TOP1α binds TTG2 promoter; top1α-10 shows R-loop accumulation and ectopic TTG2 in antipodal cells

edges.mechanism: nuclear UPF1 helicase cooperates with TOP1α to repress TTG2 in antipodal cells
edges.evidence: CoIP confirms TOP1α-UPF1 interaction; UPF1 binding at TTG2 weakens in top1α-10; upf1-1 shows ectopic antipodal TTG2

edges.mechanism: B3 TF drives maternal auxin biosynthesis
edges.evidence: MEE45 modulates maternal auxin biosynthesis and controls seed size by inducing AINTEGUMENTA

edges.mechanism: B3 TF activates ANT transcription via auxin
edges.evidence: MEE45 controls seed size maternally and regulates cell proliferation via the transcriptional activation of AINTEGUMENTA (ANT)

edges.mechanism: MADS TF directly activates E2Fa transcription
edges.evidence: SEEDSTICK Controls Seed Size via Direct Activation of E2Fa

edges.mechanism: E2F TF activates S-phase cell cycle genes
edges.evidence: STK controls cell cycle progression in developing seed coat through positive regulation of E2Fa

edges.mechanism: MADS TF promotes integument cell divisions in early seed stages
edges.evidence: STK acts as a maternal activator of growth in the earlier phases of seed development

edges.mechanism: MADS TF controls pectin methylesterase activity and cell wall maturation
edges.evidence: STK controls pectin methylesterase activity and pectin maturation, influencing seed germination

edges.mechanism: MADS TF promotes sub-dermal integument cell layer formation
edges.evidence: sub-dermal integument cell layers promoted by STK and TT16 MADS-Box TFs; tt16 stk ovules lack these layers

edges.mechanism: auxin signaling represses STK during ovule development (genetic interaction)
edges.evidence: Genetic interaction of SEEDSTICK, GORDITA and AUXIN RESPONSE FACTOR 2 during seed development

edges.mechanism: bHLH TF regulates triploid seed development maternally
edges.evidence: TT8 transcription factor controls triploid seed development maternally in Arabidopsis
TTG2 regulates many steps of tannin synthesis

edges.mechanism: WRKY TF affects cell wall extensibility via tannin
edges.evidence: mutations in TTG2 may reduce the elongation ability of the cell wall
B-type cyclins expressed shortly before and during mitosis; markers for mitosis and cell proliferation

perturbations.evidence_sentence: top1α-10 produces large seeds due to ectopic TTG2 in antipodal cells; parental dosage-dependent
upf1-1 produces large seeds maternally through ectopic antipodal TTG2
ttg2-6 produces small seeds; epistatic to top1α-10 and upf1-1
MEE45 controls seed size maternally; loss reduces seed size via reduced ANT induction
stk mutants show reduced seed size due to loss of E2Fa activation
stk tt16 ovules lack sub-dermal integument cell layers almost completely
Plants with higher expression of ANT produced larger seeds
